ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly reads 'Son Goku' and the card's character designation is 'Son Goku: Childhood' in official DBS card databases. The depicted child in orange gi with staff (Power Pole / Nyoibo) and dark spiky hair matches Goku's canonical childhood design throughout Dragon Ball (pre-Z). Verification is triple-confirmed: card name, official character tag, and signature visual identifiers (orange gi, Power Pole, child form).

trivia_bullets.text: Unbroken Dynasty Son Goku (BT4-079) is a yellow Common from Colossal Warfare (DBS-B04, July 2018). Its official character tag is 'Son Goku: Childhood' with traits Saiyan/Goku's Lineage. BT4's yellow cards were designed around Goku's Lineage — tracing Goku's family from Bardock through his childhood and beyond — making child-Goku a key component of the set's narrative identity [Source: dbs-cardgame.com/asia/strategy/series4/design-concept.php].

BT4-079's [Swap 2] skill allows a player to swap to a 'Goku's Lineage' card with energy cost 2 from hand. BT4 introduced [Swap] as a new keyword across yellow, designed specifically to chain Goku's Lineage cards together for deck consistency. The card is functionally a tutor/cycle piece that represents child Goku as the entry-point of the dynasty chain [Source: dbs-cardgame.com/us-en/strategy/series4/yellow.php and alterealitygames.com product listing for BT4-079].

The staff depicted is the Nyoibō (Power Pole), a gift to child Goku from his adoptive grandfather Gohan. It is one of Goku's earliest weapons and appears from Dragon Ball chapter 1 / episode 1. Goku's design is rooted in Sun Wukong from Journey to the West — Toriyama adapted the staff-wielding monkey king into a human child with a monkey tail [Source: CBR: Goku's Power Pole trivia; Wikipedia: Goku].
sources: cbr.com: Everything You Need to Know About Goku's Power Pole
